sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Brighton & Hove Albion have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, including a recent 2-0 defeat to Liverpool. Sunderland, on the other hand, have been slightly more consistent, securing 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, with a notable 1-0 victory over Newcastle United. Brighton's attacking output has been decent with 25 goals this season, but defensive lapses have cost them points.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: The last head-to-head between Brighton & Hove Albion and Sunderland, dating back to 2011, ended in a 0-0 draw. With limited recent data, historical matchups suggest a tightly contested fixture with neither side dominating.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Brighton rank 10th in the league with 23 points, boasting a strong offensive record (25 goals, 6th in the league) and decent ball possession (51.5%). Sunderland, positioned 8th with 26 points, have a more balanced approach with 19 goals scored and a solid defense conceding just 17 goals. Brighton's high pressing (1st in possessions won in the final third) could challenge Sunderland's backline.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Brighton will miss key players like Danny Welbeck (7 goals) and Lewis Dunk, which could impact their attacking and defensive stability. Sunderland's Wilson Isidor (4 goals) and Granit Xhaka (4 assists) are in form and could exploit Brighton's absences. Brighton's Yankuba Minteh (4 assists) remains a threat on the wing.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Outlook
sections.text: Brighton's style focuses on possession and creating big chances (37 this season), while Sunderland may rely on counter-attacks and late goals (35% of goals scored after the 60th minute). Brighton's tendency for high corner counts (average 5.1 per game) could be a factor, especially against Sunderland's less dominant set-piece defense.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Brighton's home advantage and Sofascore's prediction of a 51% chance of a home win, they are slight favorites. However, Sunderland's recent resilience and Brighton's missing players could make this a close contest. Expect a narrow victory for Brighton if they capitalize on their pressing and set-piece opportunities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Brighton & Hove Albion and Sunderland at the American Express Stadium ended in a goalless draw. Both teams showed moments of promise but failed to convert their opportunities into goals. Sunderland appeared more threatening in attack, creating a big chance and registering more shots on target. Brighton, on the other hand, struggled to penetrate the final third with the same intensity, despite holding a near-equal share of possession. Defensive discipline has been key for both sides, though Brighton’s two yellow cards indicate some frustration in their play.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half swung back and forth between the two teams. Brighton started brightly with a strong attacking spell early on, pushing forward with intent in the opening minutes. However, Sunderland quickly wrested control, dominating large periods with consistent pressure and dangerous forays into Brighton’s half. Their attacking momentum grew stronger as the half progressed, especially towards the closing stages, where they mounted significant pressure. Brighton had fleeting moments of resurgence but lacked the sustained threat to truly challenge Sunderland’s defense during these phases.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key team statistics
halftimeSections.text: Sunderland’s first-half performance was marked by a higher expected goals figure, reflecting their more clinical approach in the final third. They also entered the penalty area far more frequently and won a greater percentage of duels, showcasing their physical edge. Brighton, despite having nearly equal possession, couldn’t match Sunderland’s efficiency in attack, with fewer entries into dangerous areas and minimal big chances created. Defensively, Brighton held their own but showed vulnerability in aerial battles, losing out to Sunderland’s dominance in the air.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Standout players
halftimeSections.text: For Brighton, Olivier Boscagli stood out with a composed defensive display, while Maxim De Cuyper contributed creatively with key passes. Yankuba Minteh also showed glimpses of danger in attack. On Sunderland’s side, Enzo Le Fée was influential in midfield, dictating play with key passes and high expected assists. Daniel Ballard was a rock at the back and nearly scored with his efforts on target, though he missed a big chance. Brian Brobbey also posed a constant threat upfront for the visitors.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Areas for improvement
halftimeSections.text: Brighton needs to sharpen their attacking intent in the second half, focusing on creating clearer chances and making better use of their final third entries. Their duel-winning percentage also needs improvement to counter Sunderland’s physicality. Sunderland, while more threatening, must capitalize on their big chances, as missing opportunities could prove costly. Maintaining their dominance in possession and duels will be crucial for them to break the deadlock.
